2605.02463.title: When Stress Becomes Signal: Detecting Antifragility-Compatible Regimes in Multi-Agent LLM Systems
2605.02463.paper_summary: Multi-agent LLM systems are increasingly used to solve complex tasks through decomposition, debate, specialization, and ensemble reasoning. However, these systems are usually evaluated in terms of robustness: whether performance is preserved under perturbation. This paper studies a different question: whether semantic stress exposes structured variation that could support future antifragile learning. We introduce CAFE, a statistical framework for detecting antifragility-compatible regimes in multi-agent architectures. CAFE models a controlled expected distribution of semantic stressors, reconstructs an architecture-specific observed effective stress distribution from multi-dimensional judge signals, and compares both distributions using a distributional Jensen Gap under a convex stress potential. A positive gap does not imply immediate performance improvement; instead, it indicates a convex-expansive deformation of the observed stress distribution, suggesting that the architecture exposes learnable stress structure. We evaluate CAFE on a banking-risk analysis benchmark with five multi-agent architectures: flat, hierarchical, debate, meta-adaptive, and ensemble. Across all architectures, semantic stress reduces average judged quality by roughly one third. Yet all architectures exhibit positive distributional Jensen Gaps with bootstrap confidence intervals above zero. These results show that immediate quality degradation can coexist with statistically detectable antifragility-compatible stress geometry. CAFE is therefore not an antifragile learner itself, but a measurement layer for identifying when and where antifragility learning may be worth applying.
2605.02463.paper_summary_zh: 多代理 LLM 系統越來越多地被用來通過分解、辯論、專業化和集成推理來解決複雜任務。<lb/>然而，這些系統通常是根據穩健性來評估的：即在擾動下性能是否得以保持。<lb/>本文研究了一個不同的問題：語義壓力是否揭示了結構性變化，這可能支持未來的抗脆弱學習。<lb/>我們介紹了 CAFE，一個用於檢測多代理架構中抗脆弱兼容範疇的統計框架。<lb/>CAFE 模型控制預期的語義壓力分佈，從多維評審信號重建特定架構的觀察有效壓力分佈，並使用在凸壓力潛能下的分佈詹森差距比較這兩個分佈。<lb/>正的差距並不意味著立即的性能改善；相反，它表明觀察到的壓力分佈存在凸擴展變形，這表明該架構揭示了可學習的壓力結構。<lb/>我們在一個銀行風險分析基準上評估 CAFE，使用五種多代理架構：平面、層次、辯論、元自適應和集成。<lb/>在所有架構中，語義壓力使平均評價質量降低了大約三分之一。<lb/>然而，所有架構都顯示出正的分佈詹森差距，且自助信賴區間高於零。<lb/>這些結果表明，立即的質量下降可以與統計上可檢測的抗脆弱兼容壓力幾何共存。<lb/>因此，CAFE 本身並不是一個抗脆弱學習者，而是一個測量層，用於識別何時何地抗脆弱學習可能值得應用。
2605.02463.author: Jose Manuel de la Chica et.al.
2605.02463.authors: Jose Manuel de la Chica, Juan Manuel Vera, Jairo Rodríguez
